sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच | एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् | विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ||
transliteration: sañjaya uvāca | evam uktvārjunaḥ saṅkhye rathopastha upāviśat | visṛjya sa-śaraṁ cāpaṁ śoka-saṁvigna-mānasaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Having spoken thus on the battlefield, Arjuna cast aside bow and arrows and sat down on the chariot seat, his mind overwhelmed with sorrow.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s collapse marks the psychological climax of despair; his body obeys grief, not duty.
anchor_text: Arjuna drops weapons, paralyzed by sorrow, surrendering agency before the war begins.
psychological_state: Acute stress reaction with dissociative shutdown and abandonment of executive function.
trigger_scenario: Facing unavoidable conflict that threatens core attachments and moral self-image.
